stated_strategic_priorities: Scale the platform through transformative M&A to build a full-spectrum private markets franchise across PE, infrastructure, and real estate
Expand semi-liquid and evergreen product offerings to access private-wealth and retail capital channels (EQT Active Core Infrastructure strategy)
Industrialize value creation capabilities through proprietary digital tools (EQT Motherbrain AI platform) and operational improvement playbooks across portfolio companies

contextual_market_factors.tam_notes: European alternative AUM projected at ~$8T by 2028 (Preqin). EQT's ~$285B AUM makes it the largest European-headquartered pure-play alt manager. Second-largest PE firm globally by capital raised (after Blackstone). Fee-paying AUM of ~EUR 136B as of mid-2025.
contextual_market_factors.governance_notes: Founded in 1994 by Wallenberg family sphere (Investor AB). Listed on Nasdaq Stockholm in 2019 (one of earliest European alt manager IPOs). Single share class. Wallenberg sphere retains significant ownership (~17%) through Investor AB. CEO Christian Sinding has led since 2018. Partnership model with ~130 equity partners alongside public shareholders.
contextual_market_factors.regulatory_notes: Regulated under Swedish FSA and subject to EU AIFMD II (effective 2026). Global operations span 25+ offices across Europe, Americas, and Asia-Pacific. Luxembourg and other EU fund domiciles subject to evolving SFDR sustainability disclosure requirements. BPEA acquisition required multi-jurisdictional regulatory clearances across Asia-Pacific.
